LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT DEUTSCHE BANK SECURITIES, INC.'S AMENDED DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING: SUSTAINED IN PART AND OVERRULED IN PART. OVERRULED AS TO SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF CONTRACT AND THE SIXTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR CONVERSION. THE CLAIMS ARE NOT BARRED BY THE ENGAGEMENT LETTER, SECTION SEVEN, AND PLAINTIFF HAS ALLEGED A BREACH OF BOTH THE CONFIDENTIALITY AGREEMENT AND ENGAGEMENT LETTER. SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND THE FIRST (FRAUD), THIRD (GROSS NEGLIGENCE) AND FIFTH (17200) CAUSES OF ACTION TO PLEAD FACTS ESTABLISHING A DUTY OR BASIS FOR THE CAUSE OF ACTION APART FROM PARTIES CONTRACTUAL AGREEMENTS. SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND THE FOURTH CAUSE OF ACTION (INTERFERENCE WITH ECONOMIC ADVANTAGE) TO CLARIFY AND ELABORATE AND EXPAND UPON WHAT THE RELATIONSHIP IS THAT ALLEGEDLY HAS BEEN INTERFERRED WITH. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR #8600

Off Calendar Jul-22-2008 Continued To Jul-29-08 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 610
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:
Anne Marie Murphy, Esq., for Plaintiff and moving party: Nexent Ventures LLC
Emily Ludmir Aviad, Esq., and Raymond H. Cho, Esq., for Defendant and opposing party: Deutsche Bank Securities, Inc.

After hearing, Plaintiff Nexent Ventures, LLC.'s motion to compel further responses to request for production of documents and special interrogatories is Granted with request for production #6, 10, 12 modified to limit scope of request to communications regarding plaintiff and the acquisition until April 27, 2006.
The Court is prepared to strike the general objections as they are not authorized by the Code of Civil Procedure. Additionally, the Court is prepared to overrule and strike the blanket objections, other than based on the attorney client and attorney work product privilege. If any documents are withheld, based on privilege, they should be identified in a privilege log.
All requests for monetary sanctions are denied.

The hearing is contiued to July 29, 2008 at 9:00 a.m. by telephne to set compliance.
Commissioner: Bruce Chan. Not reported.

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-19-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES: Jason Russell, Esq. for Skadden, Arps, et al, 213-687-5000 for defendant and moving party Deutsche Securities, Inc. Anne Murphy, Esq. of Cotchett, Pitre & McCarthy, 650-697-6000 for plaintiff and opposing party Nexent Ventures LLC
RULING: ARGUED AND THE COURT MODIFIED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS TO DEFENDANT DEUTSCHE BANK SECURITIES, INC.'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT.
Demurrers overruled as to 1st, 3rd, 5th and 6th causes of action; ten days to answer.
As to the first cause of action, the decision in Warner Construction Corp. v. City of Los Angeles (1970) 2 Cal.3d 285, 294 authorizes this claim assuming there is no confidential relationship. Moreover, the Court must accept the allegations of the complaint as true and plaintiff alleges a confidential relationship and the Letter of Engagement is not necessarily in conflict with that allegation at the pleading stage.
As to the third cause of action, plaintiff pleads facts that must be construed in its favor to allege gross negligence by asserting defendant's exclusive knowledge of material facts and the concealment of those facts from plaintiff.
The Court's ruling on the 5th cause of action is as follow: Given the Court's findings as to the first and third causes of action, the same facts provide a sufficient basis to state a claim under 17200 by plaintiff's reliance on Civil Code sections 1709 and 1710(3).
The Court previously overruled the demurrer to the 6th cause of action for conversion and the facts alleged that defendant took plaintiff's confidential information for its benefit are sufficient.
The demurrer to the fourth cause of action is sustained without leave to amend for failure to allege an existing business relationship with Hollywood Video. In addition, paragraph 36 of the complaint makes clear that defendant had a financial stake in plaintiff's effort to acquire Hollywood Video. PREVAILING PARTY IS TO SUBMIT A FORM OF ORDER. (302/PJM) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY,

AMENDED DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-27-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT DEUTSCHE BANK SECURITIES, INC.'S AMENDED DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT.
APPEARANCES: ALI ABTAHI, ESQUIRE (COTCHETT, PITRE & McCARTHY, 650.697.6000) APPEARING FOR PLAINTIFF. JASON D. RUSSELL, ESQUIRE (SKADDEN ARPS SLATE MEAGHER & FLOM, 213.687.5238) APPEARING FOR DEFENDANT.
RULING: ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING: SUSTAINED IN PART AND OVERRULED IN PART. OVERRULED AS TO SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF CONTRACT AND THE SIXTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR CONVERSION. THE CLAIMS ARE NOT BARRED BY THE ENGAGEMENT LETTER, SECTION SEVEN, AND PLAINTIFF HAS ALLEGED A BREACH OF BOTH THE CONFIDENTIALITY AGREEMENT AND ENGAGEMENT LETTER. SUSTAIN WITH LEAVE TO AMEND THE FIRST (FRAUD), THIRD (GROSS NEGLIGENCE) AND FIFTH (17200) CAUSES OF ACTION TO PLEAD FACTS ESTABLISHING A DUTY OR BASIS FOR THE CAUSE OF ACTION APART FROM PARTIES CONTRACTUAL AGREEMENTS. SUSTAIN WITH LEAVE TO AMEND THE FOURTH CAUSE OF ACTION (INTERFERENCE WITH ECONOMIC ADVANTAGE) TO CLARIFY AND ELABORATE AND EXPAND UPON WHAT THE RELATIONSHIP IS THAT ALLEGEDLY HAS BEEN INTERFERRED WITH. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. (302/PJM/KD) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR #8600
